assembly_instructions: Tendon-driven, underactuated, 3D-printed four-fingered robotic hand. A rectangular palm/base plate houses a single actuator that pulls four flexible tendons through a central floating pulley tree. Each tendon runs along the underside of a printed finger (thumb opposed to three forward fingers), so a single motor input produces a conformal grasp — each finger stops independently as it meets the object. A cylindrical wrist connector bolts the base onto a robot arm; a cover plate + fasteners close out the actuator bay on top.

info.japanese_name: イェール OpenHand モデルT
info.summary: Open-source, tendon-driven, underactuated four-fingered robotic hand from Yale's GRAB Lab. Single actuator drives all fingers through a floating pulley tree for adaptive grasping.
info.description: The Model T is part of the Yale OpenHand Project — a family of open-source, low-cost, 3D-printed robotic hands designed by Aaron Dollar's GRAB Lab at Yale. The Model T pairs four underactuated fingers (one opposed thumb + three forward fingers) with a single motor that pulls four Spectra tendons through a floating pulley tree inside the palm. Because the pulley tree is mechanically floating, the tendons equalize tension across the four fingers — when one finger contacts an object, the others keep closing until they too make contact, producing a conformal grasp without any sensing. Finger joints are printed Nylon-12 'flexure' hinges rather than discrete pin joints, which drops the parts count and makes the whole hand robust to impact. All CAD, BOM, and build instructions are released under CC BY-NC 3.0 and have seeded dozens of academic variants.
info.operator: Yale GRAB Lab + the academic robotics community
info.contractor: Aaron Dollar / Yale GRAB Lab (OpenHand Project)
info.contract_date: OpenHand Project launched 2013; Model T widely built 2014 onward
info.contract_value: ≈US$500 in parts (one motor + printed shells + Spectra tendon)
info.status: Open-source, widely replicated in research labs

facts.label: Joints
facts.value: Printed Nylon-12 flexure hinges (no pin joints)

facts.label: Coupling
facts.value: Floating pulley tree equalizes tendon tension across fingers

facts.label: Construction
facts.value: Fused-deposition 3D printing + off-the-shelf motor, Spectra line

sources.title: Ma & Dollar — 'An underactuated hand for efficient finger-gaiting'

parts.name: Palm / Base Plate
parts.role: Printed Nylon-12 palm that houses the actuator and pulley tree and roots all four fingers.

parts.name: Wrist Connector
parts.role: Cylindrical adapter that bolts the hand to a robot-arm flange.

parts.name: Thumb (opposed)
parts.role: Opposed thumb (one flexure-jointed finger) reaching across the grasp plane.

parts.name: Index Finger
parts.role: Forward finger, three flexure-jointed segments printed as one part.

parts.name: Middle Finger
parts.role: Forward finger, slightly longer than the index.

parts.name: Ring Finger
parts.role: Forward finger, outboard of the middle.

parts.name: Tendon Actuator (motor)
parts.role: Single geared DC motor + spool inside the palm — pulls all four tendons simultaneously.

parts.name: Floating Pulley Tree
parts.role: Free-floating two-stage pulley that equalizes tendon tension across the four fingers.

parts.name: Tendon — Thumb
parts.role: Spectra tendon routed from the pulley tree along the underside of the thumb.

parts.name: Tendon — Index
parts.role: Spectra tendon routed along the underside of the index finger.

parts.name: Tendon — Middle
parts.role: Spectra tendon routed along the underside of the middle finger.

parts.name: Tendon — Ring
parts.role: Spectra tendon routed along the underside of the ring finger.

parts.name: Cover Plate
parts.role: Printed cover that closes off the actuator bay on top of the palm.

parts.name: Fastener (representative)
parts.role: Representative M2 bolt; the real assembly uses several around the cover-plate perimeter.
